analysis.consistency: The data is internally consistent. The structure adheres to expected D&D spell data formats, detailing name, level, school, casting mechanics, and effects.
analysis.quality: The quality is high. The description is comprehensive, detailing the primary function (duplicating other spells), alternative effects (material creation, healing, granting resistance/immunity, rerolling), and crucial drawbacks/risks associated with powerful wishes. The mechanical consequences (necrotic damage, reduced Strength, chance of permanent inability to cast Wish again) are clearly articulated.

mechanics.level: 9th level, the highest tier of spellcasting, which aligns with the spell's description as 'the mightiest spell'.
mechanics.school: Conjuration, which fits the theme of bringing things into existence or altering reality.
mechanics.components: V (Verbal only), indicating the power comes purely from spoken word, matching the narrative.
mechanics.duration: Instantaneous, except for effects that grant ongoing benefits (like 8 hours of immunity).
mechanics.primary_function: Duplicate any 8th level or lower spell without meeting component requirements.
alternative_effects_tiers: Material creation (up to 25,000 gp value, limited size).
Temporal manipulation (forcing a reroll within the last round).
mechanics.drawbacks: Significant. Casting for effects other than spell duplication incurs stacking necrotic damage per spell level for subsequent spells cast before a long rest, forces a severe temporary drop in Strength (reducing physical capability), and carries a 33% chance of permanent restriction on casting Wish again. This provides necessary mechanical weight to the most powerful spell in the game.
mechanics.class_availability: Sorcerer and Wizard, standard for high-level arcane magic.
